articles.author: Aamer Madhani
articles.description: Spirit Airlines has announced it is going out of business after 34 years. The ultra-low-cost airline known for its bright yellow planes and deep discount fares said Saturday it has started winding down operations. It said all its flights have been canceled and customer service is no longer available. The airline had been struggling financially since the COVID-19 pandemic, weighed down by rising costs and debt. Efforts to secure a government bailout failed, leaving about 17,000 jobs at risk. Spirit's closure could impact budget-conscious travelers, especially in areas like Las Vegas and Florida, where the airline had a significant presence.
articles.title: Spirit Airlines says it is going out of business after 34 years

articles.author: Sally Ho
articles.description: The collapse of the U.S.-based Spirit Airlines may mark the end of an era for travelers with a certain financial sensibility. But if you’ve been snagged in their now-defunct flight schedule, here are some things to know on how to get home, and get whole. Look for “rescue fares” and reduced prices on flights from other airlines including American Airlines, United Airlines, Delta Air Lines, JetBlue Airways, Frontier Airlines and Southwest Airlines. Save documentation and request refunds as soon as possible. Employees should be granted travel benefits to get home, and airlines are offering preferential interviews.
articles.title: What to do if you were traveling on Spirit Airlines

articles.author: Josh Boak
articles.description: President Donald Trump has taken a shine to the government taking stakes in the private sector. But his administration took a pass on bailing out troubled Spirit Airlines. The discount carrier ceased operations Saturday. Trump had said the government was interested in a stake in the carrier “if it's a good deal.” Under Trump, the government earlier invested in Intel and U.S. Steel and had made other deals that go against the traditional Republican position about Washington picking winners and losers. Critics say Trump's deals reflect his transactional nature. Others see some logic in competing against heavily subsidized Chinese manufacturers.
articles.title: Trump took a pass on Spirit Airlines after earlier private sector stakes

articles.author: Bernard Condon, Rio Yamat
articles.description: Spirit Airlines has ended its 34-year run, closing the chapter on a discount carrier that reshaped U.S. air travel. Once known for bright yellow planes and deeply discounted fares, the airline announced early Saturday it had shut down after its final flight from Detroit landed in Dallas. Spirit rose to prominence by unbundling fares and charging extra for services like bags and seat selection, a model later copied across the industry. But after years of losses, two bankruptcy filings, and a recent surge in fuel prices tied to the Iran conflict, the airline was unable to recover. About 17,000 employees have lost their jobs, many learning the news through media reports. A company spokesperson said more than 50,000 passengers were flown on its final day of operations.
articles.title: From rock-bottom fares to final flight: Spirit Airlines' journey ends

articles.author: Adam Schreck
articles.description: The United States is warning shipping companies that they could face sanctions if they pay Iran to safely pass through the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control issued the alert Friday. It adds another layer of pressure in the standoff between the U.S. and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz, which typically handles a fifth of the world's oil and gas shipments in peacetime. Iran effectively closed the strait to normal traffic by attacking and threatening to attack ships after the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Feb. 28.
articles.title: US warns shipping companies of sanctions over Iran payments

articles.author: Kirsten Grieshaber, Emma Burrows
articles.description: The German defense minister has responded to the U.S. plan to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, emphasizing the mutual benefits of the U.S. military presence in Europe. Boris Pistorius acknowledged Europe's need to enhance its security within NATO and suggests the U.S. also gains from its deployment. The planned withdrawal over the next six to 12 months reflects tensions in U.S.-Germany relations. President Trump has criticized NATO allies for not supporting his stance against Iran. The Pentagon's decision follows a review of force posture in Europe. NATO has stressed the importance of Europe investing more in defense.
articles.title: NATO's future: Germany's defense minister on US troop drawdown

articles.author: Mary Clare Jalonick, Stephen Groves, Seung Min Kim
articles.description: The White House is telling Congress that hostilities with Iran have “terminated” despite the continued presence of U.S. armed forces in the region. That message in a letter Friday from President Donald Trump effectively skirts a May 1 legal deadline to gain approval from members of Congress to continue the war with Iran. That deadline was already set to pass without action from Republican lawmakers who are deferring to the president. Trump also makes clear in the letter that the war may be far from over. The letter brings into stark relief the bold but legally questionable assertion of presidential power at the heart of Trump’s war.
articles.title: Trump contends hostilities with Iran have 'terminated'

articles.description: Expert advice on managing credit card debt, medical debt, student loans, and housing challenges. Real stories with actionable strategies for financial wellness.
articles.title: Money Happens: An audio series | AP News

articles.author: Leah Willingham, Jack Brook, Sophie Bates, Jeff Amy
articles.description: A generation of Black Americans across the South fought in courtrooms and in the streets during the Civil Rights Movement to dismantle barriers to voting. Their goal was to achieve political representation in a region scarred by the legacy of slavery and its aftermath. One of the crown jewels of that struggle was the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and this week it was hollowed out by the Supreme Court. The majority opinion described racism as a problem of the past. Others saw the decision as another example of its resurgence. The ruling could make it harder for minorities to get elected.
articles.title: A new generation faces renewed fight for racial representation in the South

articles.author: Stephen Whyno
articles.description: The 152nd edition of the Kentucky Derby will be run with 19 horses instead of the maximum 20 after a late scratch less than 12 hours before the race. The Puma was ruled out Saturday because of a swollen leg from a skin infection. The horse trained by Gustavo Delgado Sr. and son Gustavo Delgado Jr. opened at odds of 10-1 and was bet down to 8-1 by Friday. Renegade remains the favorite in the wide-open field for the opening leg of the Triple Crown that is set to go off at roughly 7 p.m. Eastern.
articles.title: Kentucky Derby is down to 19 horses after The Puma gets scratched
